rationale: In the In-Store Dynamic Factors section, the authors explicitly invoke Dhar, Huber, and Khan (2007) as prior evidence that previous purchases can increase later purchases. They operationalize this idea in their purchase-conversion model using the outcome of the last unplanned consideration, so the citation informs variable choice and hypothesis framing rather than serving as a generic background mention. Their estimated carryover effect is negative and interpreted as a reverse momentum pattern, placing their result in direct contrast to the cited paper's momentum claim in this setting.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the shopping momentum concept from Dhar et al. (2007) to motivate and test a lagged purchase-carryover effect in an in-store conversion model. It reports a reverse momentum result, thereby contrasting with and challenging the cited paper's positive momentum finding for this context.
